channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How a Northfield farmer picks which of his turkeys to have pardoned at White House
item.description: The competition to meet the president started months ago when John Zimmerman, his son and neighbors began examining 44 female turkeys from his flock for the Thanksgiving honor.
item.dc:creator: Todd Melby, MPR News

item.title: Should women serve in combat? Military experts weigh in
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Defense Department, Pete Hegseth, is facing a firestorm of backlash for voicing his belief that women should not serve in military combat roles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats strike deal to get more Biden judges confirmed before Congress adjourns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate won't hold votes on four of President Joe Biden's appellate court nominees as part of a deal with Republicans to allow for speedier consideration of other judicial nominations and bring Biden within striking distance of the 234 total judicial confirmations that occurred
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wabasha County board tries to dock prosecutor’s pay over plea bargains
item.description: The Wabasha County Board says County Attorney Matt Stinson isn’t doing his job. And if the law would let them cut his salary, they would.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Democrat Bob Casey concedes Pennsylvania Senate race to Dave McCormick, ending recount
item.description: Pennsylvania Democratic Sen. Bob Casey conceded the race to Republican Dave McCormick, putting an end to a recount in the Keystone State more than two weeks after Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Report: Minnesota Lottery needs to tighten up how it regulates retailers
item.description: Minnesota’s Office of the Legislative Auditor reports that the Minnesota State Lottery generally complies with requirements but has room to improve when it comes to how it manages retailers.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Republicans rally around Hegseth, Trump's Pentagon pick, as Gaetz withdraws for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s allies on Capitol Hill rallied around Pete Hegseth, Trump’s Pentagon pick, on Thursday even as new details surfaced about allegations that he had sexually assaulted a woman in 2017.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, STEPHEN GROVES and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hennepin County looks to spread the word of Minnesota’s red flag law
item.description: Nearly a year after Minnesota’s red flag law took effect, the Hennepin County Attorney’s Office says it’s looking to expand information and training on how it works.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Ted Cruz urges White House to halt $1.25B in 'digital equity' funds
item.description: Texas Sen. Ted Cruz urges the Biden-Harris administration to halt a $1.25 billion broadband program, calling its race-based criteria unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes bill that would allow Treasury to target nonprofits it deems to support terrorism
item.description: The U.S. House passed legislation that would give the Treasury Department unilateral authority to strip the tax-exempt status of nonprofits it claims support terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Thalia Beaty And Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Would the US win a war with China over Taiwan? US lawmakers briefed on the potential outcome
item.description: House lawmakers were briefed Wednesday about the potential outcome of a U.S.-China war over Taiwan within the next two years, as the global superpower increasingly encroaches on U.S. allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Donald Trump Cabinet will have zero policy input
item.description: Bill O'Reilly tells NewsNation's "CUOMO" that Trump just wants people in his Cabinet that will do exactly as he tells them to do.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith: Trump may have ulterior motive for Gaetz pick
item.description: Stephen A. Smith says that “under normal circumstances” Matt Gaetz would never be confirmed as the nation's new attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump chooses former acting Attorney General Matt Whitaker as NATO ambassador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said Wednesday that he has chosen former acting Attorney General Matt Whitaker to serve as U.S. ambassador to NATO, the bedrock Western alliance that the president-elect has expressed skepticism about for years.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans on House Ethics reject for now releasing report on Matt Gaetz
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Ethics Committee Republicans voted Wednesday against releasing the panel’s long-running investigation into President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for attorney general, former Rep. Matt Gaetz, the top Democrat on the panel said.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, STEPHEN GROVES and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk's DOGE commission considering mobile tax filing app: Report
item.description: The new DOGE commission is considering developing a free mobile app for tax filing, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s hunt for Treasury secretary down to the wire
item.description: The jockeying for President-elect Trump’s pick to run the all-important Treasury Department is coming down to the wire. Trump’s economic team will be tasked with imposing an aggressive tariff regime meant to shake up global trade while keeping financial markets calm amid the disruption.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-abortion groups have a to-do list for Trump
item.description: With Trump set to begin another term and Republicans in control of Congress, the anti-abortion movement is hoping to continue the gains it made during his first time in office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Hennepin County commissioners delay vote on proposal to reduce jail population
item.description: The state’s Department of Corrections ordered Hennepin County to reduce the number of inmates at some of its jails by Dec. 5.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Jessica Tisch named new NYPD commissioner: Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) --- Jessica Tisch will be only the second woman to be NYPD commissioner when she takes over the department next week, according to Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: Trump considering Hagerty for Treasury Secretary
item.description: Hagerty and Trump are known to get along well. Hagerty accompanied Trump to the SpaceX launch in Texas on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Grassley: Gaetz Ethics report's release would result in 'faster consideration' of nomination
item.description: Sen. Chuck Grassley (Iowa), the incoming Republican chair of the Senate Judiciary Committee, said Tuesday that the House Ethics Committee would help speed up the Senate’s consideration of former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) to lead the Justice Department if it releases its report on Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries: Republicans don't have a mandate
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Tuesday rejected the idea that voters delivered an Election Day mandate to Donald Trump and the Republicans, arguing that the GOP’s razor-thin majority in the House means that nothing will get done in Washington without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans fume as GOP absences help Democrats move judicial nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans aired frustrations Tuesday after Vice President-elect JD Vance and other party members skipped votes Monday, greasing the skids for Democratic-backed judicial nominees to be greenlighted as part of a final push to fill the bench with lifetime appointees before President-elect Trum
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to nominate Mehmet Oz for CMS administrator
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he is nominating television personality Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) administrator. Oz unsuccessfully ran for Senate in Pennsylvania in 2022 with Trump's endorsement and has been a Trump ally ever since.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Manhattan prosecutors ask for additional pause in Trump hush-money criminal case
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors are asking for a further pause in the criminal trial against President-elect Donald Trump in order to give both sides time to weigh the unprecedented nature of the situation.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Advocates urge Biden to dismantle migrant detention before Trump takes office
item.description: Nearly 200 migrant advocacy groups have asked President Joe Biden to dismantle U.S. immigration detention facilities before President-Elect Donald Trump takes office, and to give humanitarian parole to some migrants currently detained.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump-DeSantis complicated relationship could impact Senate pick: political expert
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis has a big decision to make if Sen. Marco Rubio is confirmed as the next Secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
